name: Pedals del Canal d'Urgell Trail
name_original: Mollerussa - Salt del Durán - Sierra de Miralcamp y Mollerussa — Ruta circular Pla d'Urgell
description: This moderate 19.4-mile mountain biking trail follows the historic Urgell Canal through the agricultural plains of Lleida.
region_intro: Mollerussa mountain bike trails traverse the diverse landscapes of the Pla d'Urgell in Lleida, characterized by flat agricultural plains and undulating terrain. The region is defined by significant waterways like the Canal d'Urgell and natural features such as the Estanque de Ivars y Vila-sana. These elements create a varied environment, offering a range of experiences from gentle, paved paths to more challenging, unpaved segments. Best mountain bike trails in Mollerussa The most popular mountain bike trail is Pedals del Canal d'Urgell Trail, a 19.4 miles (31.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the significant Canal d'Urgell, offering scenic views along its banks. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Mollerussa - Salt del Durán - Els Vilars — Ruta circular, an easy 17.2 miles (27.6 km) path. This circular route passes by the Salt del Durán waterfall, a notable natural feature. Local mountain bikers also love the Estanque de Ivars y Vila-sana — Ruta circular por el Pla d'Urgell, a 17.1 miles (27.6 km) trail leading through the Pla d'Urgell and past the Estanque de Ivars y Vila-sana, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Mountain biking around Mollerussa is defined by agricultural plains, the Canal d'Urgell, and the Estanque de Ivars y Vila-sana. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Mollerussa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Mollerussa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Mollerussa?
A: Mollerussa offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 100 routes to explore. These range from easy rides across the plains to more challenging routes with varied terrain.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for mountain biking in Mollerussa?
A: The trails around Mollerussa cater to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix, with approximately 11 easy routes, 80 moderate routes, and 11 more difficult trails, ensuring options for beginners to experienced riders.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Mollerussa?
A: Yes, Mollerussa has several easy routes suitable for families. These often feature mostly paved surfaces and minimal elevation gain, making them enjoyable for all ages. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e946081431/mollerussa-salt-del-duran-els-vilars-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mollerussa - Salt del Durán - Els Vilars — Ruta circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 17.2-mile path.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes around Mollerussa?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Mollerussa are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e946081431/mollerussa-salt-del-duran-els-vilars-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mollerussa - Salt del Durán - Els Vilars — Ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944444937/estanque-de-ivars-y-vila-sana-ruta-circular-por-el-pla-d-urgell" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estanque de Ivars y Vila-sana — Ruta circular por el Pla d'Urgell&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Mollerussa?
A: The region is rich in natural features. You can cycle along the historic Canal d'Urgell, visit the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4591004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Duran Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the biodiverse &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond&lt;/a&gt;, which is Catalonia's largest pond by surface area.

Q: Are there historical sites or attractions along the trails?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate historical elements. For instance, you can find trails that pass by sites like the Church of Sant Martí de Maldà, known for its Gothic architecture, or explore paths near the Bellpuig Castle, a 16th-century fortress.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Mollerussa?
A: The mountain biking experience in Mollerussa is highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, from flat agricultural plains to more undulating terrain, and the scenic routes along the Canal d'Urgell and around the Ivars and Vila-sana Pond.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Mollerussa?
A: Mollerussa offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures for riding. Summer can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are still enjoyable. Winter rides are also possible, especially on the flatter routes.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by lakes or ponds?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944444937/estanque-de-ivars-y-vila-sana-ruta-circular-por-el-pla-d-urgell" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estanque de Ivars y Vila-sana — Ruta circular por el Pla d'Urgell&lt;/a&gt; is a popular route that takes you directly past the large and biodiverse &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find trails near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Park&lt;/a&gt;, which features a pond.

Q: Is it possible to reach trailheads by public transport, or is parking available?
A: While specific public transport details for individual trailheads are not always readily available, Mollerussa itself is a well-connected town. Many routes, especially circular ones, often start and end in or near the town, where parking facilities are typically available. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking information.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails with good viewpoints in Mollerussa?
A: The region's diverse landscapes offer several scenic viewpoints. Routes along the Canal d'Urgell provide open vistas of the agricultural plains, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond&lt;/a&gt; is known for its picturesque sunsets and birdwatching spots, offering tranquil natural backdrops.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail in Mollerussa?
A: One of the longer routes available is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24440854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mitjana Iron Bridge – Parc de la Mitjana loop from Mollerussa&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 37 miles (59.5 km) and is classified as moderate. This route offers a substantial ride through the local landscape.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Canal d'Urgell?
A: Yes, the Canal d'Urgell is a defining feature of the region, and many routes utilize its banks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944397406/pedals-del-canal-d-urgell-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedals del Canal d'Urgell Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular mountain biking route that showcases the importance of the canal, offering scenic views along its course.
tips:
Popular destination for hiking or cycling among locals. It is a beautiful four-meter waterfall that we found in the third drought of the Urgell canal.

For years it provided electricity to a large company in the town of Mollerussa, which was the engine of its economy.

The Patano de la Bassella gives us a beautiful view, especially if we arrive at dusk.
It is located near Tossal del Infern (a small mountain of 301 meters in Pla d'Urgell)

From the Fita Alta, we have spectacular views of the entire plain of Lleida and the extensive fields of cultivation.
It belongs to the list of 100 summits of Catalonia (100 Cims FEEC).

Thanks to the recovery works, we can enjoy this historic point of interest.
This hill served as an observatory and trench during the Civil War.
We will find two information panels.

The popular reservoir in the Sierra de Mollerussa is ideal to add to our routes.
We can easily skirt it on foot or by bicycle and it is close to Fita Alta and the small Sierra aerodrome.

Idyllic place to spend the day with children, as a couple or as a family.
Under the shade of the trees, we have a picnic area, with benches, tables and barbecues.
There is also a small fenced pond with ducks and turtles.
Not far away is the Sierra de Mollerussa Rally circuit.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Pedals del Canal d'Urgell Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily follows unpaved tracks that are generally in good condition, making it suitable for mountain bikes, e-bikes, or gravel bikes. You'll find mostly flat or gentle inclines with no significant technical terrain, traversing the plains of Lleida alongside the historic Urgell Canal and its sub-canals.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's very suitable! The Pedals del Canal d'Urgell Trail is designed as a "slow" route with a low technical difficulty (rated 2 out of 5). It's particularly adapted for families and riders of all skill levels, emphasizing enjoyment and exploration over speed or strenuous challenge.

faqs.question: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers views of the agricultural plain, passes through small towns, and features points of interest such as the historic Urgell Canal itself. While the full route is extensive, highlights in the broader area include the Salt del Durán, the Civil War Observatory of Pla d'Urgell, and the Sierra Park.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride the trail is typically from March to October. During these months, you can expect milder temperatures and vibrant scenery, making for a more enjoyable experience.

faqs.question: Where does the Pedals del Canal d'Urgell Trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The full circular route typically starts and ends in Juneda, a town that is easily accessible. While specific parking details aren't provided, towns like Juneda usually offer public parking. The route also provides various support services, including bike rental and technical assistance points.

faqs.question: How can I access the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: The starting point in Juneda is easily accessible by both train and bus, offering convenient options for those preferring public transport over driving.

faqs.question: Is the Pedals del Canal d'Urgell Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there's no explicit mention of dog restrictions, outdoor trails in this region are generally dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to maintain the trail's natural beauty and respect other users.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride the Pedals del Canal d'Urgell Trail. It's an open route accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Pedals del Canal d'Urgell (45%), Ruta del canal (16%), and El Camí IT2TR2 El Camí de Ponent: de Balaguer a Montserrat (6%), among others.

faqs.question: What makes this trail unique or notable?
faqs.answer: The trail is notable for its historical significance, following the 19th-century Urgell Canal, a monumental hydraulic project. It promotes a "slow tourism" approach, encouraging riders to immerse themselves in the local culture, gastronomy, and heritage, rather than just focusing on speed.

faqs.question: Can the full route be customized for different fitness levels?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. While the full route is approximately 181 to 185 kilometers, it can be customized to be completed in 2, 3, or 4 stages. This allows riders to adjust the daily distance and intensity to match their fitness level, making it accessible for a wide range of abilities. A two-day option is more challenging due to the mileage, despite the flat terrain.

waypoints.name: Civil War Observatory of Pla d'Urgell
